name: Succulent Salmon (c) 1992 George Gilder
recipeIngredient: 1 package Herb-ox low salt chicken stock mix
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the potatoes into one-quarter inch slices and place them in a pan of cold water. Bring to boil on high heat.
2. Add the carrots after three minutes, season with salt, and reduce heat to medium.
3. Cook until tender (about six to seven minutes). Add broccoli about two minutes before the potatoes are done.
4. Drain if finished before the fish; return it to the hot pan.
5. In a ten-inch teflon pan, sauté onions over med-low heat using half the oil.
6. When onions start to brown, add the remaining oil and place the salmon fillet on the fat side down. Increase heat to medium-high.
7. Sauté until salmon turns white on the bottom. Add herbs, pepper, one-quarter cup of water, chicken stock mix, and orange juice. Lower heat and simmer for about five minutes, basting occasionally.
8. The salmon should be pink inside and not overdone. Cut the fillet in half and transfer to a plate with onions.
9. Spoon sauce over vegetables arranged in a semi-circle on the other half of the plate.
